commands.review_summary:
The branch updates the WinUI local fallback version to 0.6.0-alpha.1, preserves prerelease labels in AppVersionInfo, adds an OPENCLAW_UPDATE_CHANNEL alpha/prerelease policy for Updatum ZIP updates, and updates related docs/tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the stale local fallback and hardcoded A2UI literal: current main still uses 0.4.7 in the csproj fallback and dashboard fixture. I did not run build/test commands because this review is read-only and those commands create artifacts.

target.title: [Repo Assist] fix(version): use alpha fallback and add alpha update test channel
target.body:
🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, then revised by a maintainer to match the current alpha testing plan.*

## Summary

- Set the local-development fallback version to `0.6.0-alpha.1` instead of the stale `0.4.7` or stable `0.5.0`.
- Keep release versioning tag-driven: CI still overrides `Version` from GitVersion/tag outputs.
- Replace the hardcoded A2UI dashboard footer version with `AppVersionInfo.DisplayVersion`.
- Preserve SemVer pre-release labels in `AppVersionInfo` display/diagnostics while still stripping SourceLink build metadata.
- Add an explicit alpha update-testing channel: `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_CHANNEL=alpha` enables pre-release checks and release-list fetching.
- Force the in-app updater to ZIP assets, because portable ZIPs are the in-app update payloads; MSIX packages remain sideload/install assets.
- Document alpha update testing with isolated `OPENCLAW_TRAY_DATA_DIR` runs.

## Maintainer decision

Current `master` should use the alpha local fallback (`0.6.0-alpha.1`) because the next validation target is alpha release/update testing. This is not a routine release-file bump: release artifacts remain tag-driven through CI, and the csproj value is still only the local fallback for dev/test builds.

## Behavior proof

Release build metadata from this PR branch:

```powershell
CsprojVersion     : 0.6.0-alpha.1
ExeProductVersion : 0.6.0-alpha.1+303f45f28d58571aa15d452c3ec7b6d689441c95
ExeFileVersion    : 0.6.0.0
DllProductVersion : 0.6.0-alpha.1+303f45f28d58571aa15d452c3ec7b6d689441c95
DllFileVersion    : 0.6.0.0
AssemblyVersion   : 0.6.0.0
```

This means UI/diagnostics can show the alpha label after `AppVersionInfo` strips `+metadata`, while updater-facing file/assembly versions stay numeric.

Release-channel proof:

```powershell
v0.5.0 prerelease=false
OpenClawTray-0.5.0-win-x64.zip

v0.6.0-alpha.1 prerelease=true
OpenClawTray-0.6.0-507-win-x64.zip
```

## Validation

- `./build.ps1`
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore` (`2057 total / 2028 passed / 29 skipped`)
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore` (`868 passed`)
- `./build.ps1 -Configuration Release`
- Release assembly/file metadata proof above
- `git diff --check`

Note: this was a fresh linked worktree, so the first `--no-restore` test invocations hit the known zero-test no-op. I ran each test project once with restore/build enabled, then reran the required `--no-restore` commands and got the counts above.

Closes #570
